calendar.matters:
Petition For Court Recognition Of Existence Of Grantor Trust And Judicial Oversight Of The Administr
Off Calendar Nov-20-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gilbert Jay for Gordon Cheung and Herman Kwan (Petitioners).
Lawrence Murray for Edward Ngow (Beneficiary).
John Chew for Debbie Ngow (Beneficiary).

The Court questions why they should bring this trust under Court supervision, stated that it will not provide any additional or new powers for the Trustee, that if the Trustee wants to take control of something it will require a different petition anyway and that it is not adequate or necessary to achieve the goal. Mr. Jay stated that he feels all of the facts are articulated in his petition, that there is a related probate case (PES-18-302047) and that one of the residual primary beneficiaries is totally unresponsive to his Clients in the positions of Trustees. Mr. Jay also stated that the Probate Examiner suggested arguing Probate Code 17209, that they tried to get all of the financial records, that the Trustees cannot exercise the process expeditiously without the Court giving them the power to threaten Court action, that when they intend to file a final accounting they do want judicial approval and they cannot resolve the related probate case without determining what is in the trust. Mr. Murray suggested serving a subpoena to provide information and stated that if they don't comply, they can come to Court. Mr. Jay referred to Probate Code 17200, stated his Clients became the Trustees as of the date of death of March 8, 2018 and that they are ready to provide an accounting. Mr. Chew stated that the first order of business is the sale of or refinance of one of the trust properties to make the trust bequests and that he does not understand why it is taking so long to be achieved. The Court noted that none of this is in the petition. Mr. Jay stated that having Court supervision will stream line the process for the Trustees. Mr. Murray stated that they have hit a road block and that can't get any documents or information with no power to issue subpoenas. The Court stated that Mr. Jay can request a continuance to show good grounds for the Court to accept supervision (amend his petition), or Mr. Jay can take the petition off calendar and come back when he has something to file. Mr. Jay asked that the petition go off calendar. The Petition for Court Recognition of Existence of Grantor Trust, etc. is off calendar without prejudice to anything else that might be filed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Court Recognition Of Existence Of Grantor Trust And Judicial Oversight Of The Administr
Off Calendar Oct-23-2019 Continued To Nov-20-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
